## PR 5: Story Ledger Approval Normalizer

### Scope

Adds the Review Gate → Approval Normalizer writer substrate for:

- `ledger_user_feedback_v1`
- `accepted_story_ledger_v1`

This PR is intentionally stacked on PR #632. It should be retargeted after the upstream PRs merge.

### Why

PR 4 writes the raw Story Layer and quality report artifacts. PR 5 turns explicit Review Gate feedback into the immutable downstream story authority, while preserving the hard-stop rule that Phase 2 may only consume `accepted_story_ledger_v1`.

### In scope

- Build `ledger_user_feedback_v1` from reviewer disposition and corrections.
- Build `accepted_story_ledger_v1` only for valid accepted dispositions.
- Reject `rejected` feedback as an accepted-ledger write path.
- Block non-admin/non-operator accepted ledgers when hard fails remain.
- Allow explicit `admin`/`operator` override while preserving unresolved warnings.
- Apply user corrections only to canonical eight Story Layer keys.
- Add a pure authority assertion proving Phase 2 requires `accepted_story_ledger_v1`.

### Out of scope

- Phase 2 scoring or diagnostics.
- Support artifact writers.
- UI shell.
- Routes, workers, cron, SQL, prompts, queue wiring, or processor mutation.
- Any path that treats raw `pass1a_story_layer_v1` as Phase 2 story authority.

### Files added

- `lib/evaluation/review-gate/storyLedgerApprovalNormalizer.ts`
- `__tests__/evaluation/storyLedgerApprovalNormalizer.test.ts`

### Tests added

- Writes feedback plus accepted ledger for clean accepted feedback.
- Writes feedback only for rejected review status.
- Blocks non-admin accepted feedback when hard fails remain.
- Allows explicit admin/operator override.
- Rejects author override.
- Requires all eight layer dispositions.
- Applies corrections only to canonical Story Layer keys.
- Prevents Phase 2 story authority from raw or unapproved Story Layer artifacts.

### Reviewer note

This PR must remain the approval-normalizer substrate only. Reject any commit that introduces Phase 2 evaluation, support artifact generation, UI, route/worker/cron wiring, SQL, prompt changes, or alternate approval paths.
